data.intent_description: Receive-side input_errors on router1 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 (13 since last check, 70 observed) likely from speed/duplex negotiation mismatch or physical-layer fault; goal: stop receive errors and restore error-free RX.

plan.problem: Excessive input_errors on router1 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 indicating receive-side frame corruption; likely auto-negotiation mismatch or marginal physical layer.
plan.fix_summary: 1) Force interface to fixed 1000Mbps full-duplex to remove negotiation as cause (operational risk: may drop link if peer is 100Mbps). 2) If errors persist, replace/reseat cable and SFP. 3) Clear interface counters after remediation to confirm resolution.

messages.content:
What I observed
- R1 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 showed 70 input (receive) errors and had recently been taken admin down; oper state is now up in the latest snapshot but input_errors remain (70). OSPF over the link is stuck in INIT/Waiting.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Primary: speed/duplex mismatch — R1 is on auto-negotiation while the peer (R2) is forced to 100 Mbps / half‑duplex. This mismatch is the most likely cause of the receive errors and OSPF adjacency failure.
- Secondary (possible): prior physical-layer issue (cable or SFP) — a cable/SFP reseat/replacement was recorded and should be verified if errors persist.

Changes made
- Noted and documented physical remediation: interface description updated to "REM: replaced cable and reseated SFP per remediation step".
- Interface was cycled (admin down then up).
- An attempt to force interface parameters was made but did not apply the intended 100 Mbps/half setting — interface remains configured for auto-negotiation.

Verification — result: FAILED
- Input errors still present (input_errors = 70).
- Running-config still shows auto-negotiation on R1.
- OSPF adjacency not formed (neighbor in INIT/Waiting).

Remaining work / recommended next steps (concise)
1. Apply a matching configuration on one end only:
   - Either force R1 Gi0/0/1 to speed 100 / duplex half (manual negotiation), or change R2 to auto-negotiate (preferred: make both sides auto where possible).
2. If errors continue after matching speed/duplex, replace SFPs and cable on both ends and retest.
3. After changes, verify: get_interface_status (input_errors drop to 0), running-config (negotiation set as intended), and OSPF adjacency becomes Full.

If you want, I can apply the matching speed/duplex change on R1 now or change R2 to auto — tell me which you prefer.
